FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Media

Classes

DecodeOptions

Decode/fetch robustness policy. Defaults tuned for album art over flaky links (slow internet, transient 5xx).

DecodeScheduler

Off-thread, parallel, non-blocking, PRIORITIZED image decoder (media-pipeline.md §3). Implements the portable IImageDecoder seam the ImageCache drives: Begin(int, string, int, int, ImagePriority) enqueues into one of three priority lanes (Visible > Overscan > Prefetch) — a non-blocking write on the UI thread; a pool of N worker tasks drain the highest non-empty lane and fetch+decode CONCURRENTLY; and Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) drains finished results on the UI thread and never waits on a decode. Prioritize(int, ImagePriority) promotes a queued prefetch that just scrolled into view; Cancel(int) drops a queued/in-flight decode whose row recycled. Robustness: per-attempt timeout, transient retry with exponential backoff, permanent fail-fast (see ImageFailureKind). Under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane is dropped — never Visible. Diagnostics post to the media counter group.

DefaultImageFetcher

HTTP(S) + local-file fetcher, configured per the cross-ecosystem consensus (Flutter cache_manager, iOS URLSession, OkHttp/Coil, browsers):

  • ONE pooled SocketsHttpHandler — never new HttpClient() per request (socket exhaustion).
  • PooledConnectionLifetime recycles connections so DNS is re-resolved (stale-DNS / CDN-edge rotation fix) without IHttpClientFactory.
  • HTTP/2 with multiple connections — CDN request multiplexing; bounded MaxConnectionsPerServer.
  • Automatic decompression; per-request deadline via the token (NOT the global HttpClient.Timeout).
  • Disk-first: a persistent DiskImageCache serves instant, offline, restart-surviving hits.
  • Streams the body into an ArrayPool buffer — no per-fetch byte[].

Reuse ONE instance app-wide. Maps transport/HTTP-status to ImageFailureKind for transient-vs-permanent.

DiskImageCache

Persistent on-disk cache of ENCODED image bytes (the second tier under the in-memory GPU residency cache) — the memory+disk model every mature loader uses (Flutter flutter_cache_manager, Nuke/Kingfisher DataCache, SDWebImage). Content-addressed by a hash of the source URL, with an LRU byte budget + max-object count + stale period. It makes CDN images instant on the second view, survives app restarts, and serves offline. Stores the compressed source (≈tens of KB), NOT the decoded BGRA (which is the memory tier) — so the budget covers thousands of covers.

Structs

FetchResult

The outcome of a fetch attempt. On success it hands back a buffer RENTED FROM Shared plus the valid length — the scheduler returns it to the pool after decode, so a fetch costs no net managed allocation. On failure it carries a classified ImageFailureKind the scheduler uses to pick transient-retry vs permanent-fail.

Interfaces

IImageCodec

Portable decode seam (media-pipeline.md §3): turn encoded bytes (JPEG/PNG/WebP/…) into PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 at a target size, constrained so a huge source never materializes full-res in CPU memory (WIC IWICBitmapScaler on Windows, CGImageSource on macOS). Implemented entirely on a worker thread; the leaf's COM/objc objects never cross the scheduler seam — only POD pixels do.

IImageFetcher

Portable fetch seam (media-pipeline.md §3 "Fetch"): turn a source URI into encoded bytes off the UI thread. Maps transport/HTTP-status errors to ImageFailureKind so the scheduler can distinguish transient (network, timeout, 5xx → retried) from permanent (404, other 4xx → not retried). Honors the cancellation token (per-attempt timeout + shutdown). HTTP(S) + local file by default (DefaultImageFetcher).

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
